title: Pleasant Empanadas with Yellow Masarepa and Double-Fry KFC
description: A delicious, crowd-pleasing Uruguayan dish featuring a gooey, citrusy filling wrapped in a crispy empanada crust made with yellow masarepa and served with a side of double-fried KFC-style chicken. Perfect for a quick and easy dinner or lunch.

ingredients.name: Grass-Fed Beef Suet
ingredients.notes: Grass-fed beef suet can be found in most health food stores or made in-house.

ingredients.name: Large Onions
ingredients.notes: Yellow onions add a sweet and pungent flavor to the empanada filling.

ingredients.name: Garlic Cloves
ingredients.notes: Use fresh garlic for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Fresh Oregano
ingredients.notes: Use fresh oregano leaves for a more intense flavor.

ingredients.name: Fresh Lemon Juice
ingredients.notes: Lemon juice adds a bright and citrusy flavor to the empanada filling.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: KFC-Style Chicken Breast
ingredients.notes: Double-fry chicken breast until crispy and golden for a delicious side dish.

ingredients.name: Unsalted Butter
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality, unsalted butter for the best flavor.

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the yellow masarepa, grass-fed beef suet, large onions, garlic cloves, and fresh oregano.
Mix the ingredients until well combined, then stir in the fresh lemon juice and large egg.
Season the mixture with salt to taste, then divide it into 8 equal parts and shape each part into a ball.
Flatten each ball of dough into a disk, then place a tablespoon of the empanada filling in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling, pressing the edges together to seal the empanada, then place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with unsalted butter, then bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the empanadas are baking, heat 2 cups of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Double-fry the KFC-style chicken breast until crispy and golden, then drain on paper towels.
Serve the empanadas hot with a side of double-fried KFC-style chicken breast.
Store leftover empanadas in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
